description: User 2 (services@getsally.io): Launches pipeline from CSV for Result project targeting LATAM fashion brands. First of 3 multi-source runs. Provides draft classification prompt that MCP improves.

steps.user_prompt: I want to work on the Result project

steps.description: User provides CSV file for gathering
steps.user_prompt: I have a CSV with LATAM fashion companies. Here: /data/take-test-100.csv. Classify them as fashion brands.
user_prompt_variants: Upload this CSV to the pipeline: /data/take-test-100.csv. These are fashion companies from LATAM.
Run pipeline on this CSV file /data/take-test-100.csv — looking for fashion and apparel brands in Latin America
Here's my company list as CSV: /data/take-test-100.csv. I want to segment by fashion/apparel vs other.

steps.description: User provides a draft classification prompt. MCP should improve it to god-level.
steps.user_prompt: Use this prompt to classify: Companies that make fashion clothes in Latin America = VALID. Marketing agencies and logistics = NOT_VALID.
user_prompt_variants: Here's my classification: VALID if fashion brand/clothing manufacturer in LATAM. NOT_VALID if marketing agency, logistics, pure retail.
Classify with: fashion/apparel brands in LATAM = target. Non-fashion companies = reject.
My segmentation: look for companies that design and produce clothing, fashion accessories, or textiles. Reject agencies and tech companies.
steps.notes: MCP should take user's rough draft and build a comprehensive via-negativa classification prompt. Like the Drive JSON examples: detailed VALID/NOT_VALID criteria, edge cases, examples.

steps.description: Blacklist check — first run, so no overlap expected
Pre-filter, scrape, and analyze with improved prompt

db_checks: gathering_runs has 1 run with source_type='csv.companies.file' for Result project
no duplicate domains in discovered_companies for same project
approval_gates has 2 gates (CP1 + CP2) for this run
gathering_prompts has prompt with improved classification text

ui_checks.check: Shows the improved classification prompt with stats
